mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series 330i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 330i engine typically costs £1,500–£8,500 supply only depending on generation. M54-era engines sit at the lower end (£1,500–£3,500), while B48 units run £3,500–£8,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series 330i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which of the six engine generations your 330i has. The M54 (E46) and N52 (early E90) are widely regarded as some of BMW's most reliable engines — the M54 has documented examples exceeding 550,000 miles. The N53 (late E90) has HPFP and injector issues. The N20 (early F30) has timing chain guide failure risk. The B48 (2015+) is a significant improvement. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series 330i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide failure on the N20 (F30 2012–2015), HPFP and injector failure on the N53 (E90 2007–2011), cooling system failures on the M54 (E46), and oil leaks on the N52 (valve cover, oil filter housing). The N20 is the most critical — timing chain guides are made of cheap plastic that deteriorates.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 3 Series 330i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept £7,000 F30 330i, a £4,500 recon is 64% of the car's value — borderline. Against a tired £4,000 E90 example, the same recon exceeds our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. For B48-engined cars (post-2015), higher vehicle values make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 3 Series 330i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E46 (2000–2005) is M54B30, E90 (2005–2007) is N52B30, E90 (2007–2011) is N53B30, F30 (2012–2015) is N20B20, F30 (2015–2019) is B48B20, G20 (2019–present) is B48B20. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,200–£3,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic failure requiring a full engine replacement. At the first sign of rattling, book an inspection immediately. Many specialists recommend preventative replacement at 60,000–80,000 miles regardless of symptoms on the N20.

eraMap.title: The 330i THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Legendary naturally aspirated six — DISA, VANOS, and cooling system weak points
Magnesium-block six — reliable but leak-prone; gaskets and water pump
Direct-injection six — HPFP, injector, and carbon build-up issues
First turbocharged 330i — timing chain guide failure risk
Modular turbo four — significantly improved reliability
Modern B48 — 255 bhp, 400 Nm. Coolant leaks and PCV wear

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs, warranty claims (modern cars only)

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current B48 generation, the most common quote request today. Older generations: see the Era Map above for M54, N52, N53, and N20-specific pricing, which runs significantly lower.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 3 Series 330i engine typically takes 8–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). Six-cylinder engines (E46–E90) require more time than four-cylinder turbo units (F30–G20).

cta.label: → Compare 3 Series 330i engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain Guide Failure (F30 2012–2015)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine, described as sounding like "an electric motor on top of your usual engine noise" , VANOS/timing codes, physical movement of the chain when inspected through the oil cap .
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The N20's timing chain guides are made of plastic that deteriorates over time . Once they break, pieces drop into the oil pan and the chain can slip, leading to piston-valve contact in this interference engine . BMW's 15,000-mile oil change interval has been identified as a contributing factor — specialists recommend 8,000–10,000 mile intervals . If you hear any rattling from the engine, especially on cold start, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: N53 High-Pressure Fuel Pump & Injector Failure (E90 2007–2011)
problems.symptoms: Rough idling, stalling, noticeable drop in power, misfires, poor fuel economy .
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,500 (HPFP replacement); £1,200+ for a full set of injectors .
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; ignoring symptoms can lead to further engine damage.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The N53's high-pressure fuel pump and piezo injectors are well-documented weak points . When one injector fails, the others are often not far behind . Injector replacement requires proper coding by a specialist or BMW dealer . Use 97 RON fuel as a minimum and avoid thrashing the car from cold to help mitigate issues .

problems.title: Cooling System Failure (M54 — E46 2000–2005)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, steam from engine bay, low coolant warning.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — age-related as plastic components become brittle.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; ignoring coolant warnings can lead to head gasket failure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the coolant warning comes on, pull over and let the engine cool.

problems.recommendation: The M54's cooling system is its most critical weak point. The plastic expansion tank, water pump, and thermostat housing all degrade with age . Neglecting the cooling system can lead to catastrophic overheating . If you see any coolant warnings, have the system inspected immediately.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Essential repair — vehicle may be undriveable without it
Replace all six when one fails — coding required
Simple fix — do it immediately to avoid head gasket failure
Interference engine — piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 330i? Check These First
buyIf: * E46 M54 with cooling system recently refreshed — bulletproof engine with proper maintenance
* N52 (2005–2007) with gaskets replaced and water pump done — reliable NA six
* Post-2015 B48 (F30 LCI or G20) — significantly improved reliability over N20
* N20 with documented timing chain replacement (especially if pre-2015)
avoidIf: * 2007–2011 N53 with no HPFP or injector history — expensive repairs await
* Pre-2015 N20 without timing chain documentation — the guides will fail
* Overheating or coolant loss on any generation — can indicate head gasket failure
* Poor service history on any generation — these engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 330i badge spans a huge range of engines — from the legendary M54 six to the modern B48 turbo-four. The M54 and N52 are the most durable naturally aspirated sixes. The N53 is best avoided unless you have a repair fund. The N20 needs timing chain vigilance. The B48 (2015+) is the modern sweet spot. With the 70% BMW scrap threshold, a £4,500 recon on a £7,000 F30 330i is 64% — borderline. On a sub-£5,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare 3 Series 330i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 3 Series 330i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (N20 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Coolant Check — Check level and condition — overheating kills these engines
* Timing Chain Documentation — Any evidence of chain replacement on N20?
* HPFP/Injector History — Any replacement on N53? Keep £1,200+ in reserve
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and oil filter housing (N52/N53 common)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
